content:
Member-only story

5 min read
Just now

--

.NET | ASP.NET Core | C# | Angular | Azure | Microservices | SQL Server | CI/CD | System Design | Production Support
Recently, I came across a set of .NET interview questions that perfectly reflect this shift from theory to practical experience. These questions are commonly asked for Senior .NET Developer, Technical Lead, and Solution Architect roles.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Tim van der Kuip on UnsplashIf you’re preparing for interviews with 5–15 years of experience, the following topics deserve special attention.
1. How Is Authentication Implemented in Your Application?
Authentication is one of the first areas interviewers explore because every enterprise application requires secure access.
Rather than defining JWT or OAuth, explain your application’s authentication flow.
Topics you should discuss include:

• Login workflow.

• Identity provider.

• ASP.NET Core Identity.

• JWT authentication.

• OAuth 2.0.

• OpenID Connect.

• Claims-based authentication.

• Refresh tokens.
